text.title: card-sorting Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for card-sorting as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using 80+ cards in a single session.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cognitive fatigue sets in above 60 cards. Participants start grouping quickly without careful consideration, producing lower-quality data. Split into two separate focused studies.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Jargon tests vocabulary familiarity, not mental models. Cards must use user language. If your sitemap says "Resource Hub", the card must say "Guides and Templates".

failure-mode.description: Running closed sort without running open sort first.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Closed sort can only validate categories you already have. Open sort discovers the categories users actually expect. Running closed sort first bakes in untested assumptions.

failure-mode.description: Agent produces confident IA recommendations from fewer than 15 participants.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Below 15 participants, co-occurrence clusters are statistically unstable. Flag sample size prominently in output and label recommendations as hypotheses requiring validation.

failure-mode.description: Agent treats card sort results as final without mandating tree testing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Card sorting shows how users group content; it does not validate that users can find content in a hierarchy built from those groupings. Tree testing is always required before building.

failure-mode.description: Agents generating card lists from sitemaps will include admin/internal pages that should not be tested.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always filter to user-facing content only. Internal pages bias the study toward internal structure rather than user mental models.

failure-mode.description: Co-occurrence clustering is sensitive to card label wording — agents rephrase cards between the study design and analysis phases.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents must not rephrase cards between study design and analysis. Wording changes alter how cards cluster, invalidating the study data.

failure-mode.description: Publishing an IA recommendation from sort data alone without human review.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Business, legal, and technical constraints are invisible to the agent. Human review is required before publishing recommendations.
